notes: decimal(10,0) carries no fractional part; safely promoted to INT64. Primary key uniqueness validated after every load cycle per acceptance criteria.

Table is TIME-partitioned on hire_date at MONTH granularity. NOT NULL in source. Optimises hire-cohort and date-range analytics queries.
Cluster column (position 2). Moderate-cardinality code field; clustering accelerates job-based filtering and aggregation. Source max 20 chars.
BigQuery NUMERIC supports up to 29 digits with 9 decimal places; decimal(8,2) fits entirely without precision loss. NOT NULL in source.
Nullable in source; only applicable to sales roles. decimal(4,2) fits within BigQuery NUMERIC without precision loss.
Nullable self-referential FK to employee_id. decimal(10,0) has no fractional part; safely cast to INT64. NULL denotes a top-level employee with no manager.

Cluster column (position 1). Nullable in source; low-to-moderate cardinality identifier. decimal(4,0) has no fractional part; safely cast to INT64. Placed first in cluster order due to high selectivity for dept-level queries.
Cluster column (position 3). Low-cardinality field (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE, TERMINATED). NOT NULL in source. Source max 10 chars.
NOT NULL. Records when the row was first created in the source system. Can serve as fallback delta key if UPDATED_DATE is NULL on a given record.

Primary delta tracking column for timestamp-based incremental loads. Nullable; rows never updated carry NULL. Incremental pipeline uses COALESCE(MAX(updated_date), MAX(created_date)) as high-water mark. Null violations logged before load is marked complete per acceptance criteria.
